narrative: Fire and climate dominate today's notable findings: a new climate-emulator framework (IAM-FIRE) projects that global wildfire activity and carbon emissions could fall sharply under rapid emissions cuts but surge under stalled mitigation, while in Canada rising wildfire and harvest are flipping forests from a carbon sink to a disturbance-driven carbon source. A large multi-species analysis overturns assumptions about climate vulnerability, finding more local extinctions among temperate than tropical species, and mountain mammals and birds face shrinking ranges as they shift upslope into degraded land-use contexts. On movement and population dynamics, GPS-tracked elephants altered habitat selection and activity across drought timescales, and a 19-year, 92,000-individual dataset revealed strong spatial structure in age-dependent survival and breeding age. The strongest evolutionary signal came from a stag beetle study linking the cessation of gene flow to reduced sexual ornamentation, alongside rainfall-driven local adaptation in a widespread butterfly and rapid polygenic responses to five decades of intensive fishing in Malawi cichlids. Methods papers continue to push deep learning into ecological pipelines, from transferable joint species distribution models to a standardized activity-recognition workflow for bio-logging data, while a biogeography study of reef fish links body size and range size to global abundance patterns. In conservation, a tribute marks the passing of Pedro Sarmento, architect of Iberian lynx recovery in Portugal, alongside a global mapping of data-deficiency hotspots meant to direct future monitoring effort.

articles.title: IAM‐FIRE: A Climate Emulator–Based Framework to Project Wildfire Impacts and Risks for Integrated Assessment Models

methods_radar.title: ‘SiteTool': a ‘Shiny' application for field site selection and evaluation
methods_radar.note: Introduces SiteTool, an open-source R Shiny app for quantitative, remote-sensing-based field site selection and evaluation.

methods_radar.title: RAPID: Real‐time animal pattern re‐identification on edge devices, an open‐source tool for field deployment
methods_radar.note: Introduces RAPID, an open-source real-time animal re-identification tool deployable on low-power edge devices like drones and camera traps.

methods_radar.title: Harnessing edge computing and citizen science: A new prototype design for continental‐scale acoustic monitoring in Australia and beyond
methods_radar.note: Presents a new open-source, modular prototype combining edge computing and citizen science for continental-scale acoustic biodiversity monitoring.

methods_radar.title: A continuous‐time random encounter and staying time (REST) model: Moving beyond temporal aggregation in camera‐trap density estimation
methods_radar.note: Introduces a continuous-time REST model for camera-trap density estimation that relaxes the synchronized-activity assumption of prior methods.

methods_radar.title: Satellite hindcasts of foliar traits reveal a subtle but consistent relaxation of conservativeness in a biodiverse mountain grassland over the last four decades
methods_radar.note: Combines field trait measurements with four decades of satellite hindcasts to track functional trait shifts in mountain grassland.

methods_radar.title: Harnessing the power of machine and deep learning for transferring joint species distribution models considering the structure of biotic interactions
methods_radar.note: Benchmarks machine- and deep-learning joint species distribution models against traditional approaches for transferability across regions.

methods_radar.title: The effect of trait choice on hybrid species distribution model projections under climate change
methods_radar.note: Tests how choice of thermal-performance trait affects hybrid species distribution model projections under climate change scenarios.

methods_radar.title: Estimation of Forest Soil Organic Carbon Density in Dry Tropical Valleys Based on Multi-source Data and Machine Learning Algorithm
methods_radar.note: Applies multi-source remote-sensing data and a machine-learning algorithm to estimate forest soil organic carbon density across dry tropical valleys.

methods_radar.title: Long-Term Vegetation Changes Across the Canadian Forest-Tundra Ecotone Using Historic Air Photos and Satellite Time-Series
methods_radar.note: Combines historic air photos with satellite time-series to reconstruct four decades of vegetation change across the Canadian forest-tundra ecotone.

methods_radar.title: Satellite Remote Sensing of Alpine Vegetation Dynamics: Challenges and Perspectives
methods_radar.note: Perspective paper critiques satellite-based alpine greening trend interpretation, highlighting methodological and phenomenological complexity that can bias conclusions.

methods_radar.title: Socioeconomic traces of land use intensification in Uruguay: census meets remote sensing data
methods_radar.note: Combines census and remote-sensing data to trace land-use intensification signals, integrating existing data sources rather than introducing a new tool.

methods_radar.title: Accuracy of occurrence and abundance estimates from insect metabarcoding
methods_radar.note: Benchmarks how lysis method and spike-ins affect accuracy of insect occurrence and abundance estimates from DNA metabarcoding.

methods_radar.title: Optimal deep learning activity recognition pipeline using animal bio‐logging data
methods_radar.note: Benchmarks and optimizes deep-learning pipelines for animal activity recognition from bio-logging sensors, addressing inconsistent prior approaches.

methods_radar.title: Filtration of airborne eDNA outperforms leaf swabs for terrestrial vertebrate detection in a temperate mixed forest with longer filtration times performing the best
methods_radar.note: Compares airborne eDNA filtration times against leaf swabs for vertebrate detection, finding longer filtration performs best in forests.

methods_radar.title: Integrated analysis of fossils and molecular divergence time estimates a latest Jurassic origin of angiosperms
methods_radar.note: Integrates fossil data and Bayesian Brownian Bridge modelling to derive molecular-clock calibrations, dating angiosperm origin to the Late Jurassic.

methods_radar.title: Changing patterns of colonisation and persistence during the wolf recolonisation of the human‐dominated Italian alpine region
methods_radar.note: Applies dynamic occupancy models to multi-year wolf presence data, tracking colonisation and persistence across the Italian Alps.

methods_radar.title: Spectrally Guided Delineation of Structurally Homogeneous Vegetation Patches Using Sentinel-2 Imagery in Heterogeneous Post-Industrial Landscapes
methods_radar.note: Uses Sentinel-2 spectral imagery to delineate structurally homogeneous vegetation patches in heterogeneous post-industrial landscapes.

methods_radar.title: Cities at Sea: Coastal Urbanization Generates Local Biodiversity Hotspots but Homogenizes Marine Fish Communities Regionally
methods_radar.note: Uses seawater eDNA sampling and existing metabarcoding methods to reveal urbanization's homogenizing effect on regional marine fish communities.

methods_radar.title: Effective fine‐scale drone monitoring of wild Asian elephants for conflict mitigation and ecological research
methods_radar.note: Uses fine-scale drone monitoring to track wild Asian elephants, demonstrating feasibility for conflict mitigation and behavioural research.

methods_radar.title: Understanding variability and complexity of airborne fungal eDNA
methods_radar.note: Reviews variability and complexity of airborne fungal eDNA signals, comparing temporal accumulation dynamics to plants and vertebrates.

methods_radar.title: A fungal perspective: Ecological reinterpretation of short‐term temporal variation in airborne eDNA
methods_radar.note: Perspective reinterprets short-term airborne eDNA variation for fungal communities, synthesizing aerobiology knowledge against plant and vertebrate patterns.

methods_radar.title: DNA Metabarcoding Reveals Unexpected Predator–Prey–Microbial Dynamics in the Southern Right Whale (Eubalaena australis)
methods_radar.note: Uses DNA metabarcoding to reveal Southern right whale diet and predator-prey-microbial dynamics amid shifting foraging grounds.

methods_radar.title: Environmental temperature shapes heart size evolution in birds
methods_radar.note: Uses Bayesian phylogenetic mixed models across 915 bird species to test Hesse's rule linking heart size and climate.
